heading: Reportable events
label: 1343 Reportable events

children.heading: Notification that event has occurred
children.label: a Notification that event has occurred
children.text: Within 30 days after the plan administrator or the contributing sponsor knows or has reason to know that a reportable event described in subsection (c) has occurred, he shall notify the corporation that such event has occurred, unless a notice otherwise required under this subsection has already been provided with respect to such event. The corporation is authorized to waive the requirement of the preceding sentence with respect to any or all reportable events with respect to any plan, and to require the notification to be made by including the event in the annual report made by the plan.

children.heading: Notification that event is about to occur
children.label: b Notification that event is about to occur
A
children.text: the aggregate unfunded vested benefits (as determined under ) of plans subject to this subchapter which are maintained by such sponsor and members of such sponsor’s controlled groups (disregarding plans with no unfunded vested benefits) exceed $50,000,000, andsection 1306(a)(3)(E)(iii) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the funded vested benefit percentage for such plans is less than 90 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: a person subject to the reporting requirements of section 13 or 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 [, 78(d)], or15 U.S.C. 78mo

children.label: B
children.text: a subsidiary (as defined for purposes of such Act [ et seq.]) of a person subject to such reporting requirements.15 U.S.C. 78a

No later than 30 days prior to the effective date of an event described in paragraph (9), (10), (11), (12), or (13) of subsection (c), a contributing sponsor to which the requirements of this subsection apply shall notify the corporation that the event is about to occur.
The corporation may waive the requirement of this subsection with respect to any or all reportable events with respect to any contributing sponsor.

when the Secretary of the Treasury issues notice that a plan has ceased to be a plan described in , or when the Secretary of Labor determines the plan is not in compliance with subchapter I of this chapter;section 1321(a)(2) of this title
when an amendment of the plan is adopted if, under the amendment, the benefit payable with respect to any participant may be decreased;

when the number of active participants is less than 80 percent of the number of such participants at the beginning of the plan year, or is less than 75 percent of the number of such participants at the beginning of the previous plan year;

when the Secretary of the Treasury determines that there has been a termination or partial termination of the plan within the meaning of , but the occurrence of such a termination or partial termination does not, by itself, constitute or require a termination of a plan under this subchapter;section 411(d)(3) of title 26

when the plan fails to meet the minimum funding standards under (without regard to whether the plan is a plan described in ) or under ;section 412 of title 26section 1321(a)(2) of this titlesection 1082 of this title
when the plan is unable to pay benefits thereunder when due;

children.label: A
children.text: such distribution has a value of $10,000 or more;

children.label: B
children.text: such distribution is not made by reason of the death of the participant; and

children.label: C
children.text: immediately after the distribution, the plan has nonforfeitable benefits which are not funded;

when a plan merges, consolidates, or transfers its assets under , or when an alternative method of compliance is prescribed by the Secretary of Labor under ;section 1058 of this titlesection 1030 of this title
when, as a result of an event, a person ceases to be a member of the controlled group;

when a contributing sponsor or a member of a contributing sponsor’s controlled group liquidates in a case under title 11, or under any similar Federal law or law of a State or political subdivision of a State;

when a contributing sponsor or a member of a contributing sponsor’s controlled group declares an extraordinary dividend (as defined in ) or redeems, in any 12-month period, an aggregate of 10 percent or more of the total combined voting power of all classes of stock entitled to vote, or an aggregate of 10 percent or more of the total value of shares of all classes of stock, of a contributing sponsor and all members of its controlled group;section 1059(c) of title 26

when, in any 12-month period, an aggregate of 3 percent or more of the benefit liabilities of a plan covered by this subchapter and maintained by a contributing sponsor or a member of its controlled group are transferred to a person that is not a member of the controlled group or to a plan or plans maintained by a person or persons that are not such a contributing sponsor or a member of its controlled group; or
when any other event occurs that may be indicative of a need to terminate the plan and that is prescribed by the corporation in regulations.

children.heading: Notification to corporation by Secretary of the Treasury
children.label: d Notification to corporation by Secretary of the Treasury

children.text: whenever a reportable event described in paragraph (1), (4), or (5) of subsection (c) occurs, or
whenever any other event occurs which the Secretary of the Treasury believes indicates that the plan may not be sound.

children.heading: Notification to corporation by Secretary of Labor
children.label: e Notification to corporation by Secretary of Labor

children.text: whenever a reportable event described in paragraph (1), (5), or (8) of subsection (c) occurs, or
whenever any other event occurs which the Secretary of Labor believes indicates that the plan may not be sound.

children.heading: Disclosure exemption
children.label: f Disclosure exemption
children.text: Any information or documentary material submitted to the corporation pursuant to this section shall be exempt from disclosure under , and no such information or documentary material may be made public, except as may be relevant to any administrative or judicial action or proceeding. Nothing in this section is intended to prevent disclosure to either body of Congress or to any duly authorized committee or subcommittee of the Congress.section 552 of title 5
